summary: Applied the command catalog and approval gates live by validating the catalog on main and confirming a representative host mutation contract and approval gate before recording them as the steady-state execution path.

verification.observed: scripts/command_catalog.py --validate completed successfully against the command catalog on main.

scripts/command_catalog.py --command configure-network confirmed the expected make entrypoint, sensitive_live_change approval policy, receipt requirement, and rollback guidance for the representative host mutation.
scripts/command_catalog.py --check-approval for configure-network returned OK for a human_operator requester and approver with validation, preflight, and receipt-planning inputs marked complete.
The command catalog remains part of the repository data-model validation gate alongside workflows, receipts, control-plane lanes, and canonical stack state.

notes: This is a governance live apply: no new host mutation was required because the live platform already uses repo-managed workflows and receipts.
The representative approval check intentionally used configure-network because it exercises the higher-risk sensitive_live_change policy.
